title: Python, Tcl, Tkinter, Threads, and the Event Loop · Runtime Lens: Thread Boundary
summary: The interface creates multiple roots, blocks callbacks with work, calls widgets from an arbitrary thread, and exits with scheduled callbacks still alive. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the thread boundary increment in single-root event-loop lifecycle contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain tkinter thread routing and keep UI mutation on the owning event-loop path.
Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-runtime-tk-event-loop-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-runtime-tk-event-loop-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_runtime_tk_event_loop_2(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-thread-boundary-1beslk
# Competency: maze-thread-boundary.
# Case 1283: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_thread_boundary_1beslk(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1beslk = "1beslk-klseb1-1283"
    assert evidence_variant_1beslk
    evidence_axes_1beslk = (
        "grid1beslk",
        "topology1283",
        "searchklseb1",
        "event1beslk1283",
        "access12831beslk",
        "recoveryklseb11283",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1beslk)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Explain tkinter thread routing and keep UI mutation on the owning event-loop path.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Thread Boundary model · Theory Thread Boundary, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Thread Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Thread Boundary model · Theory Thread Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3886 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain tkinter thread routing and keep UI mutation on the owning event-loop path.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Thread Boundary model · Theory Thread Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Any Python thread can safely update a widget directly.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-thread-boundary-1beslk.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-thread-boundary-1beslk(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_thread_boundary_1beslk\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?roots != 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pending_callbacks < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
